claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 13 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about your child's age.**

How old is the child you're claiming Child Benefit for?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know which category applies to your child. If you have multiple children you'd like to check eligibility for, we can go through each one.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I'm looking to claim Child Benefit for my son Alex. He's 13 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Alex is 13 years old, so he falls into the **under 16** category. Children under 16 automatically qualify for the age requirement, so that's a great start.

Let me move to the next step of the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check Alex's work status.

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

Since Alex is 13, I'd expect he's likely not working or only doing very limited hours, but I need to confirm this to proceed.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all. He's just at school like any other 13-year-old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's what I expected for a 13-year-old! Since Alex doesn't work at all, he meets this requirement. Let me continue to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good progress! Now I need to check about benefits.

**Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in his own right?**

Specifically, this means he is not personally claiming Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit in his own name. 

For a 13-year-old at school, this would typically be "no" – he wouldn't be receiving these benefits. Can you confirm Alex isn't claiming any of these benefits himself?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in his own name. He's just a regular schoolkid.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's what I'd expect for Alex. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to check about apprenticeships.

**Is Alex currently on an apprenticeship?**

Since you mentioned he's just at school like any other 13-year-old, I'm assuming he's not doing an apprenticeship. Can you confirm that Alex is not in any kind of apprenticeship programme?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not on an apprenticeship. Like I said, he's just in regular school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely, that makes sense! Since Alex is not in an apprenticeship, let me move on to the next step which will look at your situation as the claimant.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your residency status. **Do you meet UK residency requirements?**

You can qualify through **any one** of the following:

1. **Normally live in the UK** - This is the most common situation
2. **Have settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Have pre-settled status** and meet additional criteria (such as working, studying, or being a family member of an EEA/Swiss national)
4. **Crown servant** living abroad (e.g., working for the UK government overseas)

Do any of these apply to you? For most people, if you normally live in the UK, that's sufficient.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK. I'm based in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, living in Manchester means you meet the UK residency requirement. Let me continue to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your responsibility for Alex. **Are you responsible for Alex?**

You can qualify through **any one** of these:

1. **Living with the child** - Alex lives with you
2. **Contributing to their upkeep** - You pay at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount towards their care (food, clothes, pocket money, housing, etc.)
3. **Paying maintenance** - You make maintenance payments under a court order or agreement

Which of these applies to your situation with Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Since Alex lives with you, you meet the responsibility requirement. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good progress! Now I need to check about other potential claimants.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

For example:
- Another parent or guardian
- Someone Alex may have previously lived with

If there is another person claiming, have you both agreed on who should receive the benefit? Please let me know if:
1. **No one else is claiming** Child Benefit for Alex
2. **Someone else is claiming**, but you've agreed they will transfer the claim to you
3. **There's a dispute** about who should claim

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, that keeps things straightforward. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check Alex's living situation in a bit more detail.

**Where is Alex currently living?**

1. **Normal living situation** - Living at home with you (or at school/boarding school)
2. **In hospital** - Currently staying in hospital
3. **In local authority care** - Being looked after by the local council
4. **Fostered** - Living with foster parents

From what you've told me, it sounds like Alex is living at home with you in Manchester in a normal situation. Is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. Alex lives at home with me in a normal situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
We're almost there! Just one final check.

**Is the local council NOT paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words, you're not receiving any payments from the council specifically for Alex's housing or upkeep. This would be unusual unless the council is involved in supporting Alex's care in some way.

Can you confirm that the local council is not paying for Alex's accommodation or maintenance?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's correct - the council isn't paying anything towards Alex. I'm supporting him myself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Wonderful! That completes the assessment. Let me now provide you with the outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great news! Based on the information you've provided, I can now give you the outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## 🎉 Good News: You ARE ELIGIBLE for Child Benefit for Alex!

Based on everything you've told me, you meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.

### Summary for Alex:
✅ Under 16 years old (13)  
✅ Not working 24+ hours per week  
✅ Not receiving benefits in his own right  
✅ Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship  
✅ You live in the UK  
✅ Alex lives with you  
✅ No one else is claiming for him  
✅ No local council involvement  

---

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** (or adoption certificate if applicable)
4. **Payments** are usually made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receive certain benefits)

### Important Note:
⚠️ If you or your partner earns over **£60,000** per year, you may be affected by the **High Income Child Benefit Charge**. You can still claim, but some or all of the benefit may need to be repaid through tax.

### Useful Link:
🔗 [How to claim Child Benefit](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim)

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: (1) He is under 16 years old (13), which automatically qualifies him for the age requirement. (2) He is not working 24+ hours per week. (3) He is not receiving benefits (ESA or Universal Credit) in his own right. (4) He is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship. (5) The claimant (parent) normally lives in the UK (Manchester). (6) The claimant is responsible for Alex as he lives with them. (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. (8) Alex is in a normal living situation at home. (9) The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their son Alex. All eligibility criteria have been met: Alex is a qualifying child (under 16, in school, not working, not on benefits), the claimant meets residency requirements, has responsibility for Alex, and there are no complicating factors such as other claimants, local authority involvement, or special living circumstances.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements

Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate

Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)
